human_intro: Firebase Auth gives you Google's ecosystem and 50K free users. Supabase Auth gives you open-source Postgres and RLS. I tested both with Next.js 15 to see which one actually works better in production.

seo.meta_title: Firebase Auth vs Supabase Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Choosing between Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth? We break down the differences in pricing, setup, and Next.js integration to help you decide.
seo.og_title: Firebase Auth vs Supabase Auth — I Tested Both So You Don't Have To
seo.og_description: Choosing auth for your Next.js app? Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth compared: Free – Pay-as-you-go vs Free – $75/mo, 15 minutes vs 15 minutes setup.

hero.headline: Firebase Auth vs Supabase Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: When comparing Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth for Next.js App Router authentication, the differences come down to your priorities. Supabase Auth leads with a 7/10 Next.js integration score and a easy learning curve. Firebase Auth scores 6/10 with a easy learning curve. Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo, while Firebase Auth starts at Free – Pay-as-you-go.
verdict_text: For this matchup, Supabase Auth comes out ahead. The Next.js integration (7/10) is genuinely better in practice  –  not just on paper. Firebase Auth is a good tool with its own strengths, particularly around yes, up to 50,000 mau free tier. Choose accordingly.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Massive free tier with 50,000 MAU at no cost
Tightly integrated with Firebase and Google Cloud ecosystem
Supports email/password, phone, Google, Apple, and 10+ social providers
Well-documented with abundant tutorials and community resources
Anonymous auth for gradual user onboarding
cons: Google ecosystem lock-in - less portable than open-source alternatives
No built-in Next.js App Router server component helpers
Client-side heavy - requires careful setup for SSR
Google deprecation history creates platform risk
Limited organization and role management features

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

faq.question: Should I choose Firebase Auth or Supabase Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Firebase Auth scores 6/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier or Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Firebase Auth or Supabase Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Firebase Auth starts at Free – Pay-as-you-go with a free tier of Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier. Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Firebase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Firebase Auth or Supabase Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Firebase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Firebase Auth and Supabase Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Firebase Auth or Supabase Auth?
faq.answer: Firebase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 6/10, and a user rating of 4.3★. Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, 7/10, and 4.4★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
